title: IBIA and GCMD shake hands to advance maritime decarbonization
summary: IBIA and GCMD renewed their vows, promising more research and knowledge-sharing. Expect continued industry-funded studies that don't address HFO's black carbon problem. Viable alternative fuels must also be economically viable for shipowners to avoid FuelEU Maritime penalties.
content:
A key partnership aimed at advancing maritime decarbonization has been renewed between the International Bunker Industry Association (IBIA) and the Global Centre for Maritime Decarbonisation (GCMD).

As announced on 20 April, a meeting was held in Singapore between IBIA Chair Adrian Tolson, Vice Chair Jeroen de Vos, Executive Director Alexander Prokopakis, and Professor Lynn Loo, CEO of the Global Centre for Maritime Decarbonization (GCMD). The meeting formalized the continuation of a longstanding collaboration between IBIA and GCMD, underscoring a shared goal to drive the transition toward a lower-emissions maritime industry.

Both organizations agreed to expand joint efforts in research, knowledge-sharing, and the development of practical solutions to cut greenhouse gas emissions across global shipping.

As part of the renewed partnership, the focus will include identifying viable alternative fuels, improving fuel standards, and tackling operational and regulatory barriers that challenge decarbonization progress.

The collaboration also reinforces the need for coordinated action across the maritime value chain, ensuring that emerging solutions are not only environmentally sustainable but also commercially and operationally feasible.
